ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic bagel entry (FOODON_00002683); no specific jalapeño bagel entry exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific jalapeño bagel code. Mapped to the broad bread and bakery products category A000E as the closest parent defensible anchor.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard jalapeño bagel (approximately 105–115 g) served with a typical portion of cream cheese (approximately 15–20 g), totalling around 120 g per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Jalapeño Bagel — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Jalapeño Bagel (served with cream cheese), cr_id G00011074. All per-100g values are taken verbatim from the HPB record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI value exists for a jalapeño bagel in the University of Sydney GI Database. The GI of 72 is taken from the closest analogous food: plain white bagel (GI ~72, high category) as reported in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283) and the University of Sydney GI Database. Jalapeño flavouring does not materially alter the glycaemic response of the refined wheat base. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (72) × carbohydrate per serving (62.9 g for 120 g serving) / 100 = 45.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 422 mg/100 g with low potassium offset (107 mg/100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this food; sodium at 422 mg/100 g exceeds the 400 mg/100 g caution threshold and the potassium-to-sodium ratio is unfavourable, with cream cheese spreads adding further sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate (52.4 g/100 g) with low dietary fibre (1.6 g/100 g) and high GI (~72)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should strictly limit portion size or avoid this food; the refined wheat base delivers a high glycaemic load (GL ~45 per serving) and the low fibre content (1.6 g/100 g) provides insufficient buffering of the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium (422 mg/100 g) and high refined carbohydrate load
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, the elevated sodium content (422 mg/100 g) and high refined carbohydrate load contribute to blood pressure and triglyceride risk; saturated fat is low (0.4 g/100 g) and cholesterol is 0 mg/100 g, which are favourable, but overall sodium and glycaemic burden warrant moderation.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Jalapeño (nightshade) and high refined carbohydrate pro-inflammatory load
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should exercise caution; jalapeño is a nightshade vegetable whose capsaicin and solanine content may exacerbate joint inflammation in sensitive individuals, and the high refined carbohydrate load may further drive glycaemic inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: A Jalapeño Bagel is a ring-shaped, dense, chewy bread roll made from refined wheat flour and leavened with yeast, distinguished by the inclusion of diced or sliced jalapeño peppers that impart a mild-to-moderate spicy heat and a slightly tangy flavour. The bagel is traditionally boiled before baking, which gives it its characteristic glossy crust and dense, chewy crumb. As served in Singapore food service contexts, it is typically accompanied by cream cheese, which adds a cool, creamy, mildly tangy contrast to the spice of the jalapeño. Nutritionally, the jalapeño bagel is carbohydrate-dominant at 52.4 g/100 g, with moderate protein (10.6 g/100 g) and very low fat (1.3 g/100 g). Dietary fibre is low at 1.6 g/100 g, and sodium is elevated at 422 mg/100 g. The glycaemic index is high (~72), making this a high-glycaemic-load food unsuitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes or insulin resistance without strict portion control. The sodium content places it in the caution zone for hypertension management. Iron content is notable at 3.57 mg/100 g, likely from fortified wheat flour. This food is vegetarian and halal-suitable but contains gluten and dairy (from cream cheese). It is commonly consumed as a breakfast or lunch item and is widely available in café and bakery settings across Singapore.
common_user_queries: Can diabetics eat a jalapeño bagel?
How many calories in a jalapeño bagel?
Is jalapeño bagel high in sodium?
Is jalapeño bagel suitable for hypertension?
Jalapeño bagel with cream cheese calories

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00011074) with a stated confidence of 0.95. The source is an approved primary regional source for Singapore food records. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (plain white bagel) from the University of Sydney GI Database, which is the approved GI source; the estimation basis is clearly documented.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific jalapeño bagel preparation are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry (G00011074) as of 2026-04-16. 2. saturated_fat_g (0.4 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central bagel reference data (USDA SR Legacy #18003) as the HPB record does not disaggregate fat fractions; this is a minor adaptation and does not affect core macro integrity. 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 mg/100 g as reported by HPB; this is a confirmed zero per the source — the bagel base contains no animal fat and the cream cheese contribution is averaged into the composite per-100g figure. 4. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate based on plain white bagel from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care); jalapeño flavouring does not materially alter glycaemic response. 5. This food is not a Singaporean dish by origin (it is American/Western); therefore the Singaporean multilingual naming requirement (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) does not apply. The food is sold in Singapore but its cuisine classification is American/Western.
